name: The Lazarus Chamber
description: The vats are larger than you expected. This is the thought that keeps arriving — not the horror, not the ethics, just the scale. Row on row of glass and steel, each one containing a body suspended in chemical preservation, crystal growing in slow fine patterns through the tissue, blue-white in the fluorescent dark. Some of the bodies are recent. Some are very old, the crystal overgrown to something almost architectural, something that stopped being a person and started being a structure somewhere in the middle of a century. The manifests on the wall list them by intake number, not name. You notice that the oldest intake numbers belong to bodies where the crystal has gone completely opaque — you cannot see what is inside anymore. The newest ones you can. Some of them have their eyes open. None of them are moving. You keep checking to make sure.
